name: Ginger-Pear Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cups Shredded unpeeled pears (such as Bosc or Bartlett)
recipeInstructions:
1. Spray a 6-cup fluted tube pan or a 13x9x2-inch baking pan with nonstick coating. If using a fluted pan, dust with all-purpose flour.
2. In a mixing bowl combine the sugars, oil, and egg whites. Stir mixture until well blended.
3. Add the all-purpose flour, whole wheat flour, the 2 tablespoons crystallized ginger, baking soda, and nutmeg. Stir mixture just until moistened. (Batter will be thick.)
4. Stir in shredded pears. Pour batter into the prepared pan.
5. Bake in a 350°F oven for 50 to 55 minutes for the tube pan or 25 to 30 minutes for a 13x9x2-inch pan, or until a wooden pick inserted near the center comes out clean. (For the tube pan, cool on wire rack 15 minutes before removing cake from pan.)
6. Cool completely.
7. For topping, in a bowl combine yogurt and the 1 tablespoon crystallized ginger. Cover and chill several hours before serving.
8. To serve, dollop each serving with the ginger-yogurt topping. Sprinkle with nutmeg, if desired.
